objectives: Assess test coverage and quality for the mx20022 Rust ISO 20022 library
Identify critical gaps in unit, integration, property-based, and E2E tests
Evaluate test fixture quality in testdata/
Assess scheme-specific validation tests (FedNow, SEPA, CBPR+)
constraints: Covers all 7 crates: codegen, model, parse, validate, translate, cli, mx20022 umbrella
important_facts: Total passing tests: 621 (0 failures, 4 ignored doc-tests)
Test distribution: codegen=169, validate=190, translate=132, parse=52, model=18, cli=9
No property-based testing (proptest/quickcheck) anywhere in the codebase
Coverage CI job is commented out in .github/workflows/ci.yml
Translation testdata fixtures (testdata/translation/) have only input.txt, no expected.xml output files
parse/tests/fixtures/ and codegen/tests/fixtures/xsd/multi_file/ contain only .gitkeep placeholder files
Codegen snapshot testing covers only head.001.001.04 — 8 other XSD schemas have no snapshots
pacs.002 has no roundtrip or fixture-based tests despite being generated (3 versions)
Older pacs.008 versions (001.008, 001.010) have no tests; only 001.013 is tested
head.001.001.02 has no tests at all
Scheme validators (FedNow, SEPA, CBPR+) only test pacs.008.001.13 — not pacs.002, pacs.004, pacs.028
MT940->camt.053 roundtrip is string-contains only, not a field-level structural comparison
Newtype TryFrom/new() construction validation exists but has no dedicated tests in model crate
XML malformed input: only 2 unit tests covering basic parse error in parse/src/de.rs
SWIFT MT malformed: well-covered with 6+ error-path tests in translate/tests/mt_parser.rs
No tests for: XML namespace collisions, large/huge messages, concurrent validation, locale edge cases
Benchmarks exist (parse, validate, translate) but are compile-only in CI (--no-run), never executed
doc-tests are used extensively in validate crate (30 doc-tests) — a good pattern
3 doc-tests in generated model mod.rs files are ignored (compilation issues in doc examples)
risks_issues: No coverage measurement in CI — gaps are invisible without instrumentation
Translation testdata fixtures incomplete (no expected.* output files for diff-based testing)
Codegen snapshot regression testing limited to one schema
Property-based testing absent — parser robustness for adversarial inputs unknown
pacs.002 message type untested end-to-end despite being a widely-used payment status type

iteration.summary: Performed a complete read-only audit of test coverage across all 7 crates. Found 621 passing tests with good baseline coverage in core areas (validate rules, MT parser, scheme validators, roundtrips). Critical gaps include: no property-based testing anywhere, coverage CI job commented out, translation testdata fixtures lacking expected output files, codegen snapshots covering only 1 of 9 XSD schemas, and pacs.002 having no end-to-end test despite being generated and used by all 3 scheme validators. The test pyramid has an inverted shape in the validate crate (more integration than unit) but this is appropriate given the domain.
inputs_consulted: testdata/ directory structure and fixture files
next_actions: Add proptest to validate/parse/translate dev-dependencies and implement property-based fuzzing
Create expected.xml output files in testdata/translation/ for golden-file testing
Add codegen snapshots for pacs.008.001.13 and camt.053.001.11 (the highest-value schemas)
Add pacs.002 roundtrip and scheme validation tests
Uncomment and configure coverage job in ci.yml using cargo-llvm-cov
Add tests for newtype TryFrom construction validation in mx20022-model
Add malformed XML edge case tests (truncated, bombed, namespace collision)
Fix or remove ignored doc-tests in generated camt/pacs/pain mod.rs files

mentions.relevance_note: Parse crate context — roundtrip tests were added in this iteration
Translation tests context — confirms testdata/translation/ fixtures were created during this work
